commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add --since-last-time option to recent-scrobbles (#29)

## Summary

- Add `--since-last-time` flag to `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles` to
automatically fetch only new scrobbles since the last run
- Track scrobble count persistently in `~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`
(owned 100% by spotfm)
- Every run (with or without the flag) updates the state file

## Motivation

Previously, getting new scrobbles required a manual workflow:
1. Check `data/spotify-2026.yaml` for last count
2. Check Last.FM UI for current count
3. Calculate diff manually
4. Run with `-l <diff>`

Now it's just: `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time`

## Usage

```bash
# First run — initializes state file (use -l as usual)
spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles -l 38

# Subsequent runs — fully automated
spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time
# → Fetching 38 new scrobbles (was 135, now 173).
# → Artist - Track - 5 - 10 - https://...
```

## State file format

`~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`:
```json
{
  "last_scrobble_count": 173,
  "last_run_date": "2026-03-06"
}
```

## Edge cases

- No state file yet → prints helpful message: "No previous state found.
Run once without --since-last-time to initialize."
- No new scrobbles → prints "No new scrobbles since last run." and
updates state

## Test plan

- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_lastfm.py tests/test_utils.py` — 83
tests pass
- [ ] `make test` — 262 tests pass
- [ ] `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles -l 5` — runs and creates
`~/.spotfm/lastfm_state.json`
- [ ] `spfm lastfm recent-scrobbles --since-last-time` — fetches diff
automatically

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

fix: token leak + backend freezing + attribute preservation

Problem 1 (token leak): Remove verbose logging that wrote full prompt
content for every request (177MB log in 7 days). Replace with one-line
per-request logging. Remove StreamHandler (console output).

Problem 2 (backend freezing): Add NSSM service installer BATs for
headless operation (no terminal = no QuickEdit freeze). Add watchdog.py
that checks /health every 5s and restarts backend if unresponsive.
Remove all console output from backend.

Problem 3 (attribute stripping): Rewrite patch_apply_update_message.py
to find and patch ALL occurrences of attributes = updatedMessage.attributes
instead of only 2 hardcoded patterns. Reduces catch-up from 100 to 30
messages and account switch catch-up from 50 to 10 chats.

feat: enable LLM sampling in smart-answer tool (#37)

## Why

The `@mctx-ai/mcp-server` framework just shipped `createAsk()` wiring in
v0.6.0, which gives tool handlers a working `ask` parameter when the
connected client supports sampling. The smart-answer tool already has
conditional logic to use `ask` when available, but was pinned to a
framework version that predates the feature.

## What This Does

Bumps `@mctx-ai/mcp-server` to `^0.6.0` so the smart-answer tool's
existing sampling code path is actually reachable. When connected via a
client that supports sampling (WebSocket/SSE), the tool now delegates
questions to the client's LLM and returns the response. HTTP clients
still get the graceful fallback message.

---

commit.message:
Revert to os.environ in resource — dg.EnvVar fails at ExecutionPlanSnapshot

dg.EnvVar() in resource configs is resolved at ExecutionPlanSnapshot time, which
runs on the code server gRPC process. The code server does not have Dagster+ UI
deployment env vars in its environment (only run workers do). Use os.environ.get()
in setup_for_execution instead, which runs in the run worker where those vars exist.

commit.message:
Filter for scheduled-date events only, kill all anytime-trigger markets

The core insight: we need markets where NOTHING HAPPENS until a specific
scheduled date, so price is stable/boring before the event. Markets where
the triggering event can happen at any random moment are toxic to the
strategy.

New hard filters kill:
- "by/before [date]" deadline markets (arrests, resignations, captures)
- Price targets: "hit/reach/dip to $X" — can move any moment
- "in [Month/Year]" occurrence markets
- Geopolitical unpredictable events (strikes, attacks, captures)
- "Up or Down" candle markets

Expanded keyword lists: sports (50+), awards (30+), elections (10+)
with abbreviated month names (Feb/Mar/etc).

LLM prompt rewritten to emphasize the SCHEDULED DATE test:
"Is there a specific date where all the action happens, with a
predictable quiet period before it?"

100K markets -> ~36 pass hard filter (earnings, scheduled votes,

commit.message:
fix: sign CI APK with release keystore to fix update failures

The build-apk workflow was using assembleDebug, which signs with an
ephemeral debug key unique to each CI runner. This caused signature
mismatches preventing APK updates (both sideloaded and from Play Store).
Now uses the same release keystore as the signed release workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Pass facilityId to saveDailyActuals to prevent data duplication

The Daily Actuals form was NOT passing the facilitId parameter when saving,
causing the save function to use primaryFacId instead of activeFacId. This
caused the filter (which clears old records) to fail, resulting in:

1. Loading/Pickup values ADDING instead of OVERWRITING
2. EOD values not being properly recorded

The fix ensures that the correct facilityId (activeFacId) is passed so that:
- Old records for the same date+facility are properly filtered out
- New values OVERWRITE instead of ADD
- All rail transfer types (loading, pickup, EOD) save correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use set merge for replyCount to avoid failure on missing user doc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: complete DAST job with app startup and add Slack notification to monitoring

- DAST: build + start app before OWASP ZAP scan (requires hasBuildScript)
- Monitoring: add Slack webhook notification step after smoke test
- Update gate5Needs to gate DAST on hasBuildScript

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix fuzzy name match: handle middle name mismatch

The iPhone app stores displayName as "FirstName LastName" (no middle name),
but the AI passes the full name with middle name from the roster (e.g.,
"Troy Jaxson Mikell"). The fuzzy matcher required ALL words to match,
so "Jaxson" caused a miss against "Troy Mikell".

Now tries first + last word matching when search has 3+ words and more
words than the stored name, handling middle name discrepancies.

commit.message:
Switch RSSM to residual prediction (δ_t = x_t - x_{t-1})

Model predicts frame-to-frame deltas instead of absolute AE latents:
- forward(): computes deltas internally, posterior receives δ_t, target is δ_t
- imagine(): accumulates predicted deltas to produce absolute latents for decoding
- forward() now returns h_final for convenience
- Vis scripts updated to compute deltas for context and pass x_last to imagine()

Residuals naturally emphasize dynamic content without temporal weighting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Normalize etapa values to fix status filter grouping

Raw CSV data had inconsistent casing/spelling of status values (e.g. Finalizada, Finalizado, FINALIZADAS). These were stored without normalization, causing each variant to appear as a separate filter badge and breaking exact-match filters. Added normalizeEtapa() function to map all variants to canonical values at data ingestion, so Finalizada/Finalizado/FINALIZADAS all map to 'Finalizada', all 'en ejecución' variants map to 'En ejecución', etc. This fixes filter grouping, stats accuracy, and badge deduplication. Also simplified the completed count check to only match the normalized 'Finalizada' value.

commit.message:
Fix Metal crash: prevent lock release before cleanup on task cancellation

Use explicit lock acquire/release instead of `async with` to ensure
CancelledError cannot release the inference lock before drain_and_join
completes. Add _stream_done event for fast drain when stream finished
normally, sync both generation and default Metal streams.
